---
title: "Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives"
---

# Guarantee Words, Quality Attributes, and Behavioral Adjectives

Range: `1421-1512`.

## Completion

| Status      | Count |
| ----------- | ----- |
| authored    | 19    |
| stub        | 70    |
| needs_sense | 3     |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                         | Completion  | Summary                                                                                                                                                  |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1421  | `STUB` [accessible](#rune-1421)              | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1422  | `STUB` [adaptive](#rune-1422)                | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1423  | `STUB` [amortized](#rune-1423)               | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1424  | `STUB` [approximate {Qual}](#rune-1424)      | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1425  | [atomic](#rune-1425)                         | authored    | No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.                             |
| 1426  | `STUB` [auditable](#rune-1426)               | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1427  | `STUB` [authenticated](#rune-1427)           | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1428  | `STUB` [authoritative](#rune-1428)           | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1429  | [available](#rune-1429)                      | authored    | Reachable and functioning when needed.                                                                                                                   |
| 1430  | [backward-compatible](#rune-1430)            | authored    | New versions do not casually break old clients or data.                                                                                                  |
| 1431  | `STUB` [balanced](#rune-1431)                | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1432  | `STUB` [batched](#rune-1432)                 | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1433  | `STUB` [bounded](#rune-1433)                 | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1434  | `STUB` [cache-friendly](#rune-1434)          | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1435  | `STUB` [canonical](#rune-1435)               | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1436  | `STUB` [causal](#rune-1436)                  | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1437  | [composable](#rune-1437)                     | authored    | Small parts combine without excessive friction.                                                                                                          |
| 1438  | `STUB` [concurrent](#rune-1438)              | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1439  | `STUB` [configurable](#rune-1439)            | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1440  | [consistent](#rune-1440)                     | authored    | Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.                                                                                             |
| 1441  | `STUB` [convergent](#rune-1441)              | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1442  | [crash-safe](#rune-1442)                     | authored    | Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.                                                                                                              |
| 1443  | `STUB` [declarative](#rune-1443)             | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1444  | `STUB` [deduplicated](#rune-1444)            | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1445  | [deterministic](#rune-1445)                  | authored    | Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.                              |
| 1446  | [durable](#rune-1446)                        | authored    | Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.                                                                                                             |
| 1447  | `STUB` [eager](#rune-1447)                   | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1448  | `STUB` [elastic](#rune-1448)                 | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1449  | `STUB` [encrypted](#rune-1449)               | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1450  | `STUB` [eventual](#rune-1450)                | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1451  | `STUB` [exact](#rune-1451)                   | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1452  | `STUB` [exhaustive](#rune-1452)              | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1453  | `STUB` [extensible](#rune-1453)              | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1454  | [fault-tolerant](#rune-1454)                 | needs_sense | A resilience word; some breakage is expected and survivable.                                                                                             |
| 1455  | `STUB` [federated](#rune-1455)               | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1456  | `STUB` [formal](#rune-1456)                  | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1457  | `STUB` [generic {Qual}](#rune-1457)          | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1458  | `STUB` [hardened](#rune-1458)                | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1459  | `STUB` [hermetic](#rune-1459)                | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1460  | `STUB` [high-availability](#rune-1460)       | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1461  | [idempotent](#rune-1461)                     | authored    | The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect. Shadow: duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.            |
| 1462  | [immutable](#rune-1462)                      | authored    | Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.        |
| 1463  | `STUB` [incremental](#rune-1463)             | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1464  | `STUB` [indexed](#rune-1464)                 | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1465  | `STUB` [isolated](#rune-1465)                | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1466  | `STUB` [lazy](#rune-1466)                    | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1467  | [linearizable](#rune-1467)                   | authored    | Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.                         |
| 1468  | `STUB` [lock-free {Qual}](#rune-1468)        | stub        | A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.                                                                                        |
| 1469  | `STUB` [lossless](#rune-1469)                | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1470  | `STUB` [lossy](#rune-1470)                   | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1471  | [maintainable](#rune-1471)                   | authored    | Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.                                                                                                    |
| 1472  | [memory-safe {Qual}](#rune-1472)             | authored    | A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries. Shadow: leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch. |
| 1473  | `STUB` [modular](#rune-1473)                 | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1474  | [observable](#rune-1474)                     | authored    | Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.                                                                                  |
| 1475  | `STUB` [opaque](#rune-1475)                  | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1476  | `STUB` [orthogonal](#rune-1476)              | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1477  | `STUB` [parallel {Qual}](#rune-1477)         | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1478  | `STUB` [portable](#rune-1478)                | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1479  | `STUB` [predictable](#rune-1479)             | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1480  | `STUB` [private](#rune-1480)                 | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1481  | [pure](#rune-1481)                           | authored    | Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.                                                   |
| 1482  | `STUB` [reactive](#rune-1482)                | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1483  | [reentrant](#rune-1483)                      | needs_sense | A safe-return word; the same code may be entered again before the first invocation finishes.                                                             |
| 1484  | [reliable](#rune-1484)                       | authored    | Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.                                                                                                    |
| 1485  | [reproducible](#rune-1485)                   | authored    | Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.                                                                                                 |
| 1486  | `STUB` [resilient](#rune-1486)               | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1487  | `STUB` [responsive](#rune-1487)              | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1488  | [safe](#rune-1488)                           | needs_sense | A guarded word; some class of failure is explicitly ruled out.                                                                                           |
| 1489  | `STUB` [sandboxed](#rune-1489)               | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1490  | [scalable](#rune-1490)                       | authored    | Can grow without collapsing the qualities that matter.                                                                                                   |
| 1491  | `STUB` [secure](#rune-1491)                  | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1492  | [serializable {Qual}](#rune-1492)            | authored    | Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.   |
| 1493  | `STUB` [sharded](#rune-1493)                 | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1494  | `STUB` [side-effect-free {Qual}](#rune-1494) | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1495  | `STUB` [signed](#rune-1495)                  | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1496  | `STUB` [stable](#rune-1496)                  | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1497  | `STUB` [stateful](#rune-1497)                | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1498  | `STUB` [stateless](#rune-1498)               | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1499  | `STUB` [streaming](#rune-1499)               | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1500  | `STUB` [strict](#rune-1500)                  | stub        | A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.                                                                                  |
| 1501  | `STUB` [synchronized](#rune-1501)            | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1502  | `STUB` [testable](#rune-1502)                | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1503  | `STUB` [thread-safe](#rune-1503)             | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1504  | `STUB` [traceable](#rune-1504)               | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1505  | `STUB` [transactional](#rune-1505)           | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1506  | `STUB` [typed](#rune-1506)                   | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1507  | `STUB` [validated](#rune-1507)               | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1508  | `STUB` [vectorized](#rune-1508)              | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1509  | `STUB` [verified](#rune-1509)                | stub        | A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.                                                                          |
| 1510  | `STUB` [versioned](#rune-1510)               | stub        | A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.                                                                     |
| 1511  | `STUB` [wait-free {Qual}](#rune-1511)        | stub        | A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.                                                                              |
| 1512  | `STUB` [zero-copy {Qual}](#rune-1512)        | stub        | A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.                                                                                |

## Entries

### [1421] accessible {#rune-1421}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something accessible can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1422] adaptive {#rune-1422}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something adaptive can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1423] amortized {#rune-1423}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something amortized can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1424] approximate {Qual} {#rune-1424}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something approximate can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1425] atomic {#rune-1425}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** No halfway state is allowed to leak across the line. Use it when partial success would be indistinguishable from corruption.

**Shadow:** when one layer promises indivisibility that another layer can still interleave.

**Pocket gloss:** No halfway state is allowed to leak.

### [1426] auditable {#rune-1426}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something auditable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1427] authenticated {#rune-1427}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something authenticated can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1428] authoritative {#rune-1428}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something authoritative can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1429] available {#rune-1429}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Reachable and functioning when needed.

**Shadow:** calling something available can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Reachable and functioning when needed.

### [1430] backward-compatible {#rune-1430}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** New versions do not casually break old clients or data.

**Shadow:** calling something backward-compatible can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** New versions do not casually break old clients or data.

### [1431] balanced {#rune-1431}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something balanced can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1432] batched {#rune-1432}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something batched can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1433] bounded {#rune-1433}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something bounded can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1434] cache-friendly {#rune-1434}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something cache-friendly can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1435] canonical {#rune-1435}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something canonical can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1436] causal {#rune-1436}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something causal can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1437] composable {#rune-1437}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Small parts combine without excessive friction.

**Shadow:** calling something composable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Small parts combine without excessive friction.

### [1438] concurrent {#rune-1438}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something concurrent can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1439] configurable {#rune-1439}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something configurable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1440] consistent {#rune-1440}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.

**Shadow:** calling something consistent can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Different observers are not being told incompatible stories.

### [1441] convergent {#rune-1441}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something convergent can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1442] crash-safe {#rune-1442}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.

**Shadow:** calling something crash-safe can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Failure does not leave storage in nonsense.

### [1443] declarative {#rune-1443}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something declarative can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1444] deduplicated {#rune-1444}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something deduplicated can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1445] deterministic {#rune-1445}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Given the same inputs, the same result should return. This is the antidote to drift, heisenbugs, and unreviewable behavior.

**Shadow:** when deterministic output makes a wrong assumption reproducible instead of correct.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same result.

### [1446] durable {#rune-1446}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.

**Shadow:** calling something durable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Once acknowledged, the change really sticks.

### [1447] eager {#rune-1447}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something eager can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1448] elastic {#rune-1448}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something elastic can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1449] encrypted {#rune-1449}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something encrypted can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1450] eventual {#rune-1450}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something eventual can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1451] exact {#rune-1451}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something exact can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1452] exhaustive {#rune-1452}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something exhaustive can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1453] extensible {#rune-1453}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something extensible can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1454] fault-tolerant {#rune-1454}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A resilience word; some breakage is expected and survivable.

**Shadow:** calling something fault-tolerant can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1455] federated {#rune-1455}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something federated can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1456] formal {#rune-1456}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something formal can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1457] generic {Qual} {#rune-1457}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something generic can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1458] hardened {#rune-1458}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something hardened can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1459] hermetic {#rune-1459}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something hermetic can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1460] high-availability {#rune-1460}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something high-availability can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1461] idempotent {#rune-1461}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The word that makes retries survivable: same request, same lasting effect.

**Shadow:** duplicate charges, duplicate emails, and fear of recovery.

**Pocket gloss:** Same request, same lasting effect.

### [1462] immutable {#rune-1462}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Once written, this thing does not change in place. Immutability buys clarity, replayability, and safer sharing at the cost of update convenience.

**Shadow:** when immutability is shallow and hidden references still mutate underneath.

**Pocket gloss:** Written once, then never changed in place.

### [1463] incremental {#rune-1463}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something incremental can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1464] indexed {#rune-1464}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something indexed can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1465] isolated {#rune-1465}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something isolated can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1466] lazy {#rune-1466}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something lazy can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1467] linearizable {#rune-1467}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Every operation behaves as though it took effect at one real instant visible to all observers. Use it rarely, but mean it fully.

**Shadow:** when a linearizable claim ignores the boundary, clock, or failure mode where order breaks.

**Pocket gloss:** Behaves as though each operation took effect at one visible instant.

### [1468] lock-free {Qual} {#rune-1468}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A progress word; some thread always moves forward without a lock.

**Shadow:** calling something lock-free can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1469] lossless {#rune-1469}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something lossless can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1470] lossy {#rune-1470}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something lossy can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1471] maintainable {#rune-1471}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.

**Shadow:** calling something maintainable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Cheap enough to understand, modify, and keep healthy.

### [1472] memory-safe {Qual} {#rune-1472}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A promise that the system will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

**Shadow:** leaks, corruption, and security bugs born from illegal touch.

**Pocket gloss:** Will not casually trespass across allocation boundaries.

### [1473] modular {#rune-1473}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something modular can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1474] observable {#rune-1474}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.

**Shadow:** calling something observable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Structured so internal behavior can be inferred from external evidence.

### [1475] opaque {#rune-1475}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something opaque can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1476] orthogonal {#rune-1476}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something orthogonal can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1477] parallel {Qual} {#rune-1477}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something parallel can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1478] portable {#rune-1478}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something portable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1479] predictable {#rune-1479}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something predictable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1480] private {#rune-1480}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something private can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1481] pure {#rune-1481}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden state smuggled in from the side. Use it to shrink reasoning scope.

**Shadow:** when a pure-looking function reads hidden state, time, randomness, or external effects.

**Pocket gloss:** Same inputs, same output, no hidden side effects.

### [1482] reactive {#rune-1482}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something reactive can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1483] reentrant {#rune-1483}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A safe-return word; the same code may be entered again before the first invocation finishes.

**Shadow:** calling something reentrant can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1484] reliable {#rune-1484}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.

**Shadow:** calling something reliable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Keeps doing the promised thing under ordinary stress.

### [1485] reproducible {#rune-1485}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.

**Shadow:** calling something reproducible can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Can be rebuilt or rerun and yield the same result again.

### [1486] resilient {#rune-1486}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something resilient can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1487] responsive {#rune-1487}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something responsive can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1488] safe {#rune-1488}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** needs_sense

**Force:** A guarded word; some class of failure is explicitly ruled out.

**Shadow:** calling something safe can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1489] sandboxed {#rune-1489}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something sandboxed can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1490] scalable {#rune-1490}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Can grow without collapsing the qualities that matter.

**Shadow:** calling something scalable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

**Pocket gloss:** Can grow without collapsing the qualities that matter.

### [1491] secure {#rune-1491}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something secure can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1492] serializable {Qual} {#rune-1492}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Concurrency forced to behave as though transactions happened in one clean order. Expensive when overused, indispensable when history must remain sane.

**Shadow:** when serializable language hides weaker isolation, contention, or retry behavior.

**Pocket gloss:** Concurrency behaves as though transactions happened in one clean order.

### [1493] sharded {#rune-1493}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something sharded can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1494] side-effect-free {Qual} {#rune-1494}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something side-effect-free can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1495] signed {#rune-1495}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something signed can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1496] stable {#rune-1496}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something stable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1497] stateful {#rune-1497}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something stateful can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1498] stateless {#rune-1498}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something stateless can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1499] streaming {#rune-1499}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something streaming can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1500] strict {#rune-1500}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A promise adjective; it binds the system to a stronger form of conduct.

**Shadow:** calling something strict can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1501] synchronized {#rune-1501}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something synchronized can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1502] testable {#rune-1502}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something testable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1503] thread-safe {#rune-1503}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something thread-safe can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1504] traceable {#rune-1504}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something traceable can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1505] transactional {#rune-1505}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something transactional can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1506] typed {#rune-1506}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something typed can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1507] validated {#rune-1507}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something validated can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1508] vectorized {#rune-1508}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something vectorized can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1509] verified {#rune-1509}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A property word; it says what kind of behavior should remain true under stress.

**Shadow:** calling something verified can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1510] versioned {#rune-1510}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A virtue word; it names an operational quality that can guide design and evaluation.

**Shadow:** calling something versioned can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1511] wait-free {Qual} {#rune-1511}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A guarantee word; it claims a behavioral law rather than a mere preference.

**Shadow:** calling something wait-free can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.

### [1512] zero-copy {Qual} {#rune-1512}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A nearness-and-efficiency word; data moves without redundant duplication.

**Shadow:** calling something zero-copy can hide the missing invariant, test, or operating envelope.
